registry_note: Logical interview groups as DATA, not file layout. Each group declares which interview question-IDs feed it (input_question_ids), which foundation-doc fields it derives (target_fields), the step after which all its inputs are captured (close_after), its confirmation marker, the foundation doc(s) rendered at its barrier (preview_docs), and the conditional question-IDs whose valid-skip counts as satisfied (skip_satisfied_if). The numbered interview files remain physical CARRIERS; the state machine and disk-first cold-resume are preserved. vision and approach_roster are the high-stakes narrative groups the operator most needs to validate as rendered drafts; they confirm early. The operational groups confirm at their natural late close step.
auto_global_fields_note: auto-class fields (mechanical fill at generation time) that are available early and are included in EVERY group's barrier preview inputs, so the strict single-doc preview renderer does not fail-fast on a global placeholder. These are NOT owned by any group.

special_behaviors: vision constraints elevated to top-level policy (policy class)
voice-and-style seeding (synthesis class; from literacy/info-preference/verbosity/style answers + the operator's vision voice)
agent-intent derivation (agent roster answers -> agent intents + resource claims)
AUTONOMY_LEVEL is a provisional auto-default carrying a decision-field flag and a revisit trigger (the operator authority profile is pending)
HITL_MAP_ROWS is a policy-class rule set with explicit negative permissions
